[BIOMETRIC REPORT - 25##-11-19-18-49-58-4587]
<>
ENTITY CLASS: Biological
ENTITY SUBCLASS: Sawian
ENTITY NAME: Experiment
<>
[CONTENTS]

The stable biological version of our newest project is ready.
It appears that, mentally, there wasn't and will be nothing left of our original human volunteer, not to mention the rest of the subjects.
Despite that, they seem to be learning fast, and are not retaliating.
<>
The DNA combination caused the Experiment to be considerably smaller in stature than a human person, and lack fat and muscle mass.
Despite this, it's muscle and bone density is much higher. This will assumbly result in the specimen being resilient to various forms of physical trauma... And give it a hard time swimming.
It has three protrusions at the back of its skull, of form similar to ear cartilage. Somehow, it has an ear structure similar to that of an avian - must've been lying around somewhere in the DNA we spliced.
It is thinly furred, and has pink-ish yellow-ish flesh, as seen on wounds and on the inside of the mouth.
Its hands are some kind of combination between a canine's and a homosapiens'. I know one of my co-workers is going to enjoy the paws-
Somehow, the bioluminescent eyes from our earlier prototypes of this species have stayed. We are yet to determine the exact effects this has on their sight.
<>
We will soon clone this specimen 30 times to conduct destructive testing on the clones. We will leave a few to naturally reproduce, to save on resources.

[END]

[EMAIL - 25##-12-26-14-27-52-0189]
<>
SUBJECT: Announcement on befriending Sawians
FROM: Project Manager
TO: All Employees
<>
[CONTENTS]

Dear staff,

It has come to our attention that one of our employees had been consistently meeting up with a SAW-03 and disclosing private information to it. Not only was this an egregious breach of confidentiality, but having such relationships with the specimens is completely unacceptable. The scientist in question was swiftly terminated and the SAW-03 committed suicide shortly after.

Do YOU have any Sawian friends? We are closing in on you. It's not too late to turn back. But if we catch you befriending them, you will meet the same fate.

Yours in good faith,
Project Manager

[END]

[EMAIL - 25##-12-29-13-10-50-0119]
<>
SUBJECT: Too late for your questions
FROM: Researcher
TO: Psychologist
<>
[CONTENTS]

I am writing to let you know that all of the Sawians that rioted last week have ALREADY been euthanized, so I'm afraid you will not be able to get your questions in.

They were already interviewed thoroughly, and they confessed that everything they knew came from that SAW-03, so the mystery is solved. Concerningly, some of the scientists they killed were their own handlers, and they admitted to targeting them on purpose. 

I heard security will be tightened up, so hopefully this mess will not happen again.

Regards,
Researcher

[END]

[BIOMETRIC REPORT - 25##-04-01-15-29-18-1058]
<>
ENTITY CLASS: Biological
ENTITY SUBCLASS: Sawian
ENTITY NAME: Meat-Consumption Report
<>
[CONTENTS]

Reduction of Corporate Waste Objective
<>
32 Sawians were recruited randomly among those at our site fro<corrupt:0xFFFFFF> Baseline characteristics were: 25 SAW-01, 4 SAW-05, 3 SAW-12. The subjects were randomly assigned to 8 groups of 4 subjects each. Each group were subject to one choice in three types of conditions: consuming Sawian or Terran meat, consuming raw or as soup, and observing or being ignorant of the source of the meat.

Meat was sourced from the Indicent on [25##-12-30].
<>
While the SAW-01 and SAW-05 in all groups which consumed the Sawian meat, regardless of whether they observed the source of the meat, displayed extreme negative emotion upon consumption, the SAW-12 did not display the same negative emotions. No Sawians which consumed the terran meat displayed negative emotions, presumably as they did not recognize the meat as their own, although complaints were raised about eating noncooked food. No Sawians which consumed the meat in soup form displayed negative emotions, even if they had observed the source.

The SAW-12 displayed an aberrant apathy towards consuming raw Sawian meat, as expected. We might be able to repurpose discarded specimens as food for any SAW-12s on site.

[END]

So.
This is my "journal" now. Which is apparently a written work describing one's life and experiences.
I am not entirely sure why the coats have me doing this, but the same can be said about most things they make us do.
Still, I got lucky today. It can get way worse than a writing exercise.
I find this device quite fascinating, and it is a shame that I will part with it in just a few hours. But I have been given a task, and I shall complete it.<>

We usually are woken up either by eachother, or by the first food buzzer ringing. Rarely do we wake up to testing immediately.
I'm not sure what "hour" this happens at... Hell, I barely understand the concept. Though, I hear the coats talking about it all the time...

Anyway, we wake up and soon it's time for our very first meal of the day.
What they feed us is slightly varied, which wasn't the case just a few years ago. Still mostly just nutrient bars, though. They're really bland, but I can't complain...

--------------------------------------------------<>
It's some hours later now.

This is the time when the coats hunt.
They call you out by the number, and you have to come to them.
Or they come for you. 
You do not want that.
Sometimes, they call out multiple numbers... It's usually a good sign, though.<>

What they'll do to you is up to chance, really.
The tests can be harmless, they can be terrible, or they can be beneficial.
It's worth noting that the coats merely TRY to do things, and while sometimes they try to help, those attempts are usually far less successful than the ones where they try to mess you up.<>

Two numbers are called, and two others stand up, their faces worried.
One of them is healthy. Far more likely to get hurt.

The other has four arms.
One on the left, and three on the right.
Well, if you could call the weird growths arms. They look kinda close?
They were originally born with two. The coats decided that to be too much one day, and removed one.
The next day, they came back with ELEVEN arms.
The humans removed ten of those, only to grace them with seven new ones.
The next day it was five.
Today it's four.

They are getting close now.<>

The poor fellow is called Arms. And I swear, this was their name BEFORE this whole ordeal, and it's widely believed that humans picked them because of that.
Perhaps it's their idea of a joke?

Bedtime soon after that.
